audit_items.proof_trace: Three unchecked allocation sites dereference NULL on malloc/ber_memalloc failure: (1) tool_print_ctrls L2694 `ptr = str = malloc(len+1)` immediately followed by unconditional `lutil_strcopy(ptr, ctrls[i]->ldctl_oid)` at L2698 with no NULL guard; (2) print_prepostread L2004 `str = malloc(...)` followed by `lutil_strncopy(ptr=str, ...)` at L2007; (3) print_paged_results L2078 `ptr = ber_memalloc(...)` followed by `strcpy(ptr, buf)` at L2081. All three are genuine null pointer dereferences that crash the client tool on allocation failure.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker-operated malicious LDAP server sends responses containing many or large control values to exhaust the connecting client's heap; when tool_print_ctrls is subsequently invoked to display a server control, malloc(len+1) returns NULL, and the unchecked pointer is passed directly to lutil_strcopy, causing a null pointer dereference crash (client-side DoS).
